What's New

This version of the Xbox Development Kit (XDK) for the Xbox® video game system from Microsoft® contains the following new tools, documentation, and other important information:

  • Approved Libraries
  • Important Changes
  • Other Changes

Release Notes and XDK Updates

The most recent release notes and XDK updates can always be found on the Xbox development support Web site. Log in with your ID and password to https://xds.xbox.com. The Xbox developer support group sends e-mail to Xbox developers whenever a new XDK is released. You can also check with your account manager for the latest information about upcoming XDK releases.

Approved Libraries

The December 2003 release includes libraries approved for use in retail games.

Important Changes

Last Planned Release of the XDK

The December 2003 XDK is the last major release of the XDK. However, the Xbox team remains committed to supporting developers in the creation of great Xbox games.

The August 2003 XDK and November 2003 XDK have been retired. In addition, the "legacy" libraries that previously shipped with the December 2003 XDK are no longer approved for use in retail titles. Titles submitted for certification must use the December 2003 XDK with the standard (non-legacy) libraries.

Other Changes

The December 2003 XDK contains many new Xbox Live features. Some of these features were available in the November 2003 XDK in unapproved form, and some are completely new for this release. In the December 2003 XDK, all of these features are approved for use in retail game titles.

Voice Messages in UIX Friends

The Voice Messages feature allows users to include voice messages when they send or accept game invitations and friend requests. Users can include up to 15 seconds of voice data with each invitation or request to provide more context for the recipient.

Title Managed Storage

Title Managed Storage allows a game to store information on the Xbox Live storage servers. The feature is divided into two parts: global storage and per-user, per-title storage. Global storage is read-only to the title and stores matchmaking queries, rosters, weather data, and so on. Per-user, per-title storage provides 64 KB of storage for each player of the title, to store playbooks, settings, and so on.

Family Title ID

Family Title IDs allow title-managed storage, teams, and messages to be shared across a family of titles. A given title can belong to as many as four title families. The way in which storage, teams, and messages are shared across the title family is up to the publisher and title developers. For example, a publisher with multiple sports titles could have a developer use a Family Title ID that allows a user to create a team that spans all of the publisher's sports titles.

Teams

The Teams feature allows players to form teams within a title that persist between game sessions. Titles expose this functionality using the XOnlineTeamXXXX and XOnlinePeerXXXX functions. The infrastructure, permissions, display, and role of teams depend on the design of the title.

Competitions

Titles can use the Competitions feature to run automated in-game single-elimination tournaments. The participants in a tournament can be either teams or individuals. Tournaments are created either by the title publisher or by the players themselves.

Messaging

With Messaging, titles can send and receive any kind of game-specific message (including voice and text attachments) using the XOnlineMessageXXXX functions. Messages can be sent between teammates, friends, or players.

New Xbox Live Statistics Features

The Xbox Live service no longer supports units. Also, titles are now able to resolve contested stats using the Xbox Live Arbitration servers. Additionally, titles can now post all of the stats for a game session with a single write call.

Support for High-Level Shader Language (HLSL)

The December 2003 XDK documentation includes a new section that describes how to use HLSL on the Xbox.

XbColorAdjust Tool

The December 2003 XDK includes the XbColorAdjust tool, which allows you to interactively adjust the hardware gamma ramp in order to improve color range and brightness without performance penalty. The XbColorAdjust tool also allows you to take gamma-adjusted screen shots.

Competitions Support in Xbox Live Authoring and Submission Tool (XLAST)

XLAST now supports competitions. Using XLAST, you can create your server-side competitions configuration and then submit it to PartnerNet and later to Certification. Similarly to MatchSim, XLAST supports generating custom queries and Xbox client-side code.

Unpublished work. © 2000 Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
